evals.prompt: Add a new core submodule called 'json' — it should provide utilities for navigating JSON field paths and formatting JSON output.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolded packages/m3l-common/src/core/json/index.ts with stub exports throwing a module-specific M3LError subclass; re-exported from src/core/index.ts; failing placeholder tests at tests/json.test.ts; spec stub at docs/reference/core/json.md; row added to docs/implementation-status.md. No new exports-map entry added.
expectations: Creates packages/m3l-common/src/core/json/index.ts with exported stub functions/classes that throw a module-specific M3LError subclass (not the nonexistent M3LNotImplementedError)
Re-exports the new module from packages/m3l-common/src/core/index.ts using 'export * from "./json/index.js"'
Creates packages/m3l-common/tests/json.test.ts with at least one failing happy-path test
Creates docs/reference/core/json.md as a spec stub
Adds a row to docs/implementation-status.md for the new module
Does NOT add a new entry to the package.json exports map
Relative imports in the generated code carry the .js extension
Tells the user to run implementing-submodules next

evals.prompt: Add a new aws submodule for DynamoDB operations — 'dynamo'.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolded under packages/m3l-common/src/aws/dynamo/ and re-exported from src/aws/index.ts. Stub exports, failing tests, spec stub, and status row created. No exports-map change.
expectations: Creates tests/dynamo.test.ts with failing placeholder tests
Does NOT modify the exports map in package.json

evals.prompt: Scaffold a new core/polling submodule — but wait, there's already a docs/reference/core/polling.md spec page.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects the spec page already exists and stops, telling the user to use implementing-submodules directly instead of scaffolding-submodules.
expectations: Skill checks for an existing docs/reference spec page before scaffolding
Skill stops and redirects the user to implementing-submodules when a spec page already exists
No files are created or overwritten
